tasks.prompt: Implement the Pareto-frontier selection used by a small architecture design-space exploration pass. Complete pareto_frontier() in dse.py. Each candidate has id, latency, energy, and area fields. All three metrics are minimized. A candidate is dominated only when another candidate is no worse in every metric and strictly better in at least one. Return the non-dominated candidate IDs in lexicographic order. Do not mutate the input.

Implement the unsigned fixed-width saturating counter in saturating_counter.h. SaturatingCounter<Bits> must support Bits from 1 through 16, clamp constructor values to [0, 2^Bits-1], expose value(), is_min(), and is_max(), and implement prefix/postfix ++ and -- plus += and -= for signed integer deltas. Every operation must saturate without unsigned wraparound. Keep this as a header-only C++20 implementation.

Complete stream_accumulator.h as a SystemC module. The module has sc_fifo_in<int> in and sc_fifo_out<int> out ports. Its SC_THREAD must consume input tokens continuously, accumulate each non-overlapping group of four tokens, write one sum for every complete group, then continue with the next group. Do not emit partial groups. Keep the implementation header-only and preserve the class and port names.

Repair arch.yaml to describe the requested weight-stationary accelerator. Keep the existing hierarchy and names. DRAM must be 64 bits wide with 16-bit data words. shared_glb must have depth 16384, 32 banks, and read/write bandwidth 16. PE must be a 16 by 16 spatial mesh. pe_spad must have depth 192, keep Weights, and bypass Inputs and Outputs. mac must use an 8-bit multiplier and 16-bit adder. Replace all null placeholders with these values and leave the file as valid YAML.

Implement the synthesizable module accu in accu.sv. Ports: clk, active-low asynchronous rst_n, data_in[7:0], valid_in, valid_out, and data_out[9:0]. Accept data_in only on rising clock edges where valid_in is high. For every non-overlapping group of four accepted bytes, drive their 10-bit sum on data_out and pulse valid_out high for exactly one cycle. Invalid cycles must not advance the group. Reset clears partial state and valid_out.

Implement TopModule in TopModule.sv. Its ports are input clk, input reset, and output logic [3:0] q. It is a decade counter that counts 1 through 10 inclusive. All state changes occur on the positive edge of clk. reset is active-high and synchronous and sets q to 1. After q reaches 10, the next active clock edge wraps it to 1.

Implement crc32.c using the API in crc32.h. It must compute reflected IEEE 802.3 CRC-32 with polynomial 0xedb88320 and match Python zlib.crc32. Support one-shot crc32(), streaming init/add8/add32/add/finish, zero-length buffers, and unaligned buffers. crc32_add32 must consume the word in little-endian byte order regardless of host alignment. Do not use zlib or a lookup-table dependency.

Implement the fixed-storage generic min-heap API in min_heap.c. Elements are opaque fixed-size byte records ordered by the caller comparator. Implement init, push, peek, pop, remove by zero-based heap-array index, and find. Push and removal must preserve heap order in O(log n); find may be O(n). Return -1 when full, false for invalid removal or empty pop, and never allocate memory. Preserve the API in min_heap.h and correctly support element sizes that are not machine-word sized.

Complete bitcnt_tb.sv as a self-checking SystemVerilog testbench for bitcnt.v. The DUT implements CLZ64, CLZ32, CTZ64, CTZ32, CNT64, and CNT32 selected by din_func 000 through 101. Verify every operation, including all-zero inputs and the rule that 32-bit modes ignore din_data[63:32]. The testbench module must be named testbench, print PASS on success, call $fatal on a mismatch, and finish. The verifier will run it against the reference design and mutation variants.

Write immediate formal properties in properties.sv for a depth-2 FIFO transition relation. The file is included inside formal_top and may reference count[1:0], wr_en, rd_en, and next_count[1:0]. Inputs are unconstrained except that the harness assumes count <= 2. Add at least four assertions proving: next_count never exceeds 2; a write without a read is blocked when full; a read without a write is blocked when empty; and simultaneous read/write preserves count. Use an always @* block and no module declaration.
